incubation.notes: Isolated the bag to a different room (bay 2b)

Yesterdays issue still persists.
Yesterdays issue still persists.

Noticed some bags are not colonizing but there is no indication of contamination. I.e green mould etc. Honestly this situation is unique in the sense that we have bags that are almost fully colonized and this that have barely started, yet they underwent the same preparation process.

Noticed some bags are not colonizing but there is no indication of contamination. I.e green mould etc. Honestly this situation is unique in the sense that we have bags that are almost fully colonized and this that have barely started, yet they underwent the same preparation process.

Some bags have fully colonised while others are dormant, but no contamination was observed i.e green/pink moulds. I'm suspecting a bacterial/yeast infection, which has a cottony texture of the mycelium in the spawn as it colonises the substrate. NM spawn seems to be the most affected by this. Telling whether a spawn is infected by yeast or bacteria is really difficult with the naked eye, as these microbes can attach themselves to the Mycelium without any visible colour change.  Sour smell is the only visible symptom of the millions of bacteria you cannot see. Also sometime if given enough time mushroom mycelium can fight through it if given enough time and that's why I don't want to treat this as contamination just yet, but as an infection.
